parameters.content:
## Auto-post new blog articles from RSS to social media with PostStack

### How it works
Whenever your blog (or any RSS/Atom feed) publishes a new article, this workflow builds a social post from its title, snippet and link, then publishes it through [PostStack](https://poststack.techskills.academy) - a self-hosted social media publishing and automation platform - to every channel you list: Facebook, Instagram, YouTube and more, one API. The feed trigger deduplicates on its own, so each article is posted exactly once. No Buffer, no Zapier tax - your blog markets itself from your own infrastructure.

### Setup
1. In PostStack create an API key (**Settings → API keys**). API access is a PostStack Pro feature.
2. Copy the channel ids you want to post to from **Channels** (or `GET /api/v1/channels`).
3. Fill in **Configuration (EDIT ME)**: PostStack URL, API key, comma-separated channel ids and a fallback image URL (most platforms require media; the feed item's own image wins when present).
4. Point **New Feed Item** at your feed URL and activate.

### Customization tips
Edit **Build Caption** to change the post format (pure title + link by default, snippet optional). Want an AI-written teaser in your brand voice? Drop an OpenAI node between **Build Caption** and **Get Channels** - see the "AI Social Media Manager" PostStack template for the exact pattern.

## Catch the Article
Fires once per new feed item and turns it into a ready-to-post caption.

## Publish via PostStack
Resolves your channels, creates one post per channel and publishes each - all through the PostStack REST API.

nodes.name: New Feed Item
nodes.notes: Point at your blog's RSS/Atom feed. The trigger remembers seen items - each article fires exactly once.

nodes.name: Configuration (EDIT ME)
nodes.notes: channelIds: comma-separated ids from PostStack -> Channels. imageUrl: fallback image - most platforms require media on a post.

nodes.name: Build Caption
nodes.notes: Post format lives here: title + snippet + link. Trim or extend to taste.

nodes.name: Build Posts per Channel
nodes.notes: One output item per target channel, with the platform resolved from the PostStack API.

nodes.name: Prepare Publish
nodes.notes: Pairs each created post with its target channel. URLs built here - n8n template validation dislikes multi-part expressions in URL fields.

nodes.name: Publish Now
nodes.notes: Change when to an ISO timestamp to schedule instead of publishing immediately. Pair with the post-alerts workflow to get notified about the outcome.
